description: The April 2022 referendum in Mexico on whether President Andrés Manuel López Obrador should remain in office. It was a test of the incumbent's popularity, passing with 91.9% support but failing to hit turnout thresholds.

key_events.description: Opposition parties call for an electoral boycott of the referendum.
Supreme Court limits the President's ability to promote the event.
Polls show high support but low probability of meeting the 40% threshold.
Election day shows strong partisan turnout but widespread voter apathy.

calibration_notes: Ideal for studying the disconnect between high approval and low participation in electoral stunts.
